name: Pipeline Agents
description: 27 agent definitions that form the multi-stage development pipeline — from planning through review to implementation.
notes: Each agent is a Markdown file with YAML frontmatter (name, description, model, tools). Agents are loaded by Claude Code on invocation. The pipeline sequence is: brainstormer → planner → researcher → gotcha-checker → reviewers (plan stage), then coder → completeness-checker → reviewers (implement stage), then implementer → documenter (apply stage).

capabilities.text: Plan features via brainstormer + planner + researcher + gotcha-checker + reviewers
Implement features via coder + completeness-checker + reviewers
Apply changes to source files via implementer + documenter
Debug issues via debug agent + reviewers
Refactor code via refactor agent + reviewers
Audit project structure via architect agent
Multi-reviewer code review (safety, logic, style, performance, boundary)

name: Slash Commands
description: 17 user-facing commands for pipeline control, project management, and status.
notes: Commands live in commands/forge/*.md. User types /forge:plan, /forge:implement, etc. Each command is a Markdown prompt that orchestrates one or more agents. Commands reference agents by name — Claude Code resolves them from the agents/ directory.

capabilities.text: Plan a feature with brainstormer + planner
Implement a planned feature with coder + reviewers
Apply approved implementation to source files
Debug a bug with structured analysis
Initialize a new project with FORGE pipeline files
Show project pipeline status
Manage TODO items on the board

name: Hooks
description: Session tracking, context monitoring, workflow guard, and role enforcement hooks.
notes: Hooks are Node.js scripts declared in hooks/hooks.json. They fire on Claude Code events (SessionStart, PreToolUse, PostToolUse). Scripts receive JSON on stdin and communicate via stdout/stderr/exit code. Paths use ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} for portability.

capabilities.text: Track context window usage and emit checkpoint signals
Log all tool calls to audit file for post-run analysis
Block writes that violate pipeline workflow rules
Enforce per-agent write-path restrictions via agent-roles.json

name: Parallel Sessions
description: Git worktree-based isolation for running multiple pipeline sessions concurrently.
notes: bin/forge-worktree.js manages worktree lifecycle (create, list, merge, cleanup). Each worktree gets isolated .pipeline/, docs/, .claude/ directories. bin/forge-status.js reads state from all worktrees to show combined progress in the terminal status line.

capabilities.text: Create isolated git worktree for a pipeline session
Merge completed worktree back to main branch
Show combined progress across all active sessions

name: Project Templates
description: Scaffold templates for initializing new projects with FORGE pipeline files.
notes: Templates are directory trees copied by /forge:init. Each template type (code, code-csharp, power-automate, instructional, etc.) provides starter CLAUDE.md, docs/, and .pipeline/ files tailored to that stack. The architect agent then customizes GENERAL.md and modules.json for the specific project.

capabilities.text: TypeScript/JavaScript project template with full pipeline scaffold
C# project template
Power Automate project template
Non-code instructional project template
